channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Your Amazon shop will soon cost more — 3.5% 'fuel and logistics-related surcharge' added due to Iran war knock-on effects
item.description: Amazon has increased some logistics costs in the US, Canada, Mexico and Brazil by 3.5% amid rising fuel costs.

item.title: Data Centers Create Heat Islands Stretching 6 Miles, Study Finds
item.description: Heat islands can worsen air pollution and increase heat-related deaths.

item.title: Cette bonne montre connectée sous Wear OS qui valait 279 € à sa sortie ne coûte plus que 87 € aujourd’hui
item.description: Deux ans après sa sortie, la OnePlus Watch 2R n’a rien perdu de son intérêt : cette montre Wear OS mise sur une autonomie record, un bel écran AMOLED et des performances solides. Désormais affichée à moins de 100 euros, elle devient l’une des montres connectées les plus recommandables du moment.

item.title: Apple’s best product ever
item.description: All week, we've been asking you to help us rank the 50 best products Apple ever made, as we mark the company's 50th anniversary. Thanks to everyone who pitched in - we ended up with more than 1.6 million votes! We also have lots of other coverage of Apple's first half century, and you should […]

item.title: Les Gafam qui scannent nos conversations en ligne ? C’est terminé à partir du samedi 4 avril
item.description: Depuis 2022, les géants du numérique comme Meta, la maison mère d'Instagram, pouvaient scanner nos conversations en ligne à des fins de recherche de contenus pédopornographiques. Cette possibilité expire ce vendredi 3 avril à minuit, et le Parlement européen a choisi de ne pas la renouveler : une nouvelle dont s'est félicitée la société civile, qui déplorait un « contrôle de masse non ciblé des conversations en ligne privées » ne permettant pas de davantage protéger les mineurs....

item.title: Comment installer un modèle LLM type ChatGPT sur PC ou Mac en local ? Voici le guide ultime pour tous
item.description: Et si vous aviez votre propre IA, 100 % locale, sans internet et privée ? Ce guide vous montre comment faire tourner un LLM sur votre PC, même sans être un pro.

item.title: Engadget Podcast: How Apple keeps redefining personal computing at 50
item.description: For a 50-year-old company, Apple remains pretty hip and nimble. This week, Devindra and Senior Reporter Igor Bonifacic dive into Apple's big birthday, the state of the company today and what the next 50 years could bring. It remains one of the few PC companies that’s still firmly committed to the idea of personal computing. Also, we celebrate the successful launch of NASA's Artemis II mission, which will bring us back to the Moon (but just for a close look).Subscribe!iTunesSpotifyPocket CastsStitcherGoogle PodcastsTopicApple at 50: Why it’s still all about personal computing – 1:16Artemis II ...

item.title: EU cyberattack may have been worse than we thought - 90GB of data published online as 30 entities hit
item.description: CERT-EU is blaming TeamPCP for the attack, saying the Trivy breach trickled down.

item.title: The Best Samsung Galaxy S26 Cases (2026): S26, S26+, and S26 Ultra
item.description: Protect your new Samsung phone with the help of these cases and screen protectors.

item.title: Steam Deck 2 : la date de sortie est encore loin, mais c’est une bonne nouvelle
item.description: La date de sortie du Steam Deck 2 parait encore bien loin, Valve privilégiant un véritable bond technologique plutôt qu'une simple mise à jour.

item.title: Les panneaux solaires vieillissent beaucoup mieux que prévu (et c’est prouvé)
item.description: On doute souvent de la longévité réelle des installations photovoltaïques. Pourtant, le démantèlement d'une centrale solaire vieille de plus de vingt ans vient balayer les idées reçues : avec un rendement toujours supérieur à 85 %, la technologie s'avère bien plus robuste que prévu. Mais un obstacle économique inattendu menace de les envoyer directement à la broyeuse. Explications. ...

item.title: Google Meet débarque sur CarPlay : plus d’excuse pour rater une réunion
item.description: Google Meet arrive dans votre voiture. Version strictement audio, fonctions réduites au minimum. Le conducteur n'a plus d'échappatoire.

item.title: Illumination Is Officially Learning Nothing
item.description: With 'The Super Mario Galaxy Movie' on pace to out gross 'The Super Mario Bros. Movie,' there's zero incentive to change the formula.

item.title: I’m TechRadar’s 4K Blu-ray tester — here are the 4 new discs I’m most excited about reviewing in April 2026
item.description: April 2026 is set to be another month packed with great new 4K Blu-ray releases, so I've picked the top 4 I'm most looking forward to testing.

item.title: Fan fiction website AO3 is finally coming out of beta
item.description: The famous fan fiction website Archive of Our Own or AO3 has finally exited open beta, 17 years after it launched way back in 2009. AO3 is a nonprofit created by the by the Organization for Transformative Works. In an announcement, the team reminisced about its early days and how volunteers had to manually send out invitations to prospective writers. Upon launching the website on open beta, it only had 347 accounts and hosted 6,598 works. Now, it has 10 million registered users and is hosting 17 million fan-created works. The team has highlighted some of the most useful features it has added o...

item.title: Chatbots are now prescribing psychiatric drugs
item.description: Utah is allowing an AI system to prescribe psychiatric drugs without a doctor. It's only the second time the state - and the country - has delegated this kind of clinical authority to AI. State officials say it could bring costs down and ease care shortages, but physicians warn the system is opaque, risky, and […]

item.title: 'I think EmDash was created to sell more Cloudflare services': WordPress co-founder Matt Mullenweg gives his verdict on Cloudflare's EmDash
item.description: WordPress co-founder Mullenweg isn't too critical about EmDash or Cloudflare, says he sees it as a commercial opportunity, not a 'spiritual successor'.

item.title: The Best iPad to Buy (and Some to Avoid) in 2026: Compare the Air, Pro, Mini
item.description: We break down the current iPad lineup to help you figure out which of Apple’s tablets is best for you.

item.title: The web can still be wonderful, and Flipboard’s Surf proves it
item.description: This new app lets human beings weave together worthwhile stuff from across the internet, in a way that’s uniquely rewarding—and sometimes even beautiful. Hello again, and welcome back to Fast Company’s Plugged In.

item.title: Attention, les virements bancaires ne fonctionnent pas ce week-end : voici deux alternatives
item.description: Pendant tout le week-end, du vendredi 3 avril au lundi 6 avril 2026, les systèmes européens de paiement interbancaires sont à l'arrêt. Les virements ne fonctionnent plus. Il existe heureusement des alternatives.

item.title: L’autonomie est le cadet des soucis du Oppo A6x en promotion, qui propose en plus de la 5G à très bon prix
item.description: D'abord lancé en Inde fin 2025, l'Oppo A6x 5G débarque en France à un prix attractif de 199,90 euros, en baisse à 179,90 euros sur le site de la marque, un téléphone à l'autonomie XL.

item.title: The Morning After: NASA’s Artemis II is on a voyage around the Moon
item.description: NASA’s Artemis II successfully launched on April 1, with its crew on a 10-day mission to circle the Moon. It’s the first crewed Artemis flight and a major step toward humanity returning to our little neighbor in the future. Since launch, the vehicle has separated from its launch system and been manually piloted, testing how the Orion capsule will dock with future lunar landers. There have been some snags, however: The onboard toilet went awry, and Microsoft Outlook has been acting screwy. Jokes aside, there is something magnificent about seeing humanity taking to the stars once again. That, fo...
